heading: Connect to Your Cluster
body:
- Select `mongosh` from the dropdown menu.
  We recommend that you upgrade to the latest version
  of the shell. To check the installed version of the
  [mongosh](mongodb-shell), run:

  ```sh
  mongosh --version
  ```

- *Step 2* of the Atlas modal displays a copyable
  connection string that includes the name of the
  MongoDB user that can authenticate with the cluster. Copy
  this string. To connect as a different MongoDB user,
  change the [--username](mongodb-shell/reference/options)
  option.

- Paste the [mongosh](mongodb-shell) command and connection string into a
  terminal. Run the command. The shell prompts you for the
  [password](mongodb-shell/reference/options).

> **Note:** If the input device is not a terminal, [mongosh](mongodb-shell) doesn't prompt for a password.
> Instead, [mongosh](mongodb-shell) interprets any input on stdin after the connection string as a password.
